title: Food & Dining
description: Ordering food, specifying tastes, kitchen vocabulary, and paying

lessons.title: Ordering Food
lessons.culturalNote: Traditional Maharashtrian restaurants serve 'Thali' (meals on a plate). Asking for menus and recommending items.

phrases.english: Please give me the menu card
Give me one plate of Misal Pav

lessons.title: Taste & Preferences
lessons.culturalNote: Marathi food can range from mild to very spicy. Words like 'tikhat' (spicy) and 'god' (sweet) are essential.

phrases.marathi: मला कमी तिखट जेवण हवं आहे
phrases.transliteration: Malaa kami tikhat jevaN havam aahe
phrases.english: I want a less spicy meal
I do not like spicy/greasy food

lessons.title: Table Requests
lessons.culturalNote: Asking for utensils, condiments, or extra servings.

phrases.english: I've been waiting for an hour

lessons.title: In the Kitchen
lessons.culturalNote: Understanding cooking verbs and kitchen vocabulary.

lessons.title: Paying the Food Bill
lessons.culturalNote: Concluding your dining experience with the bill request and tipping.

phrases.english: Make the payment with my card
The food was wonderful, thank you

title: Directions & Travel
description: Asking directions, taking public transit, buying tickets

lessons.title: Asking Directions
lessons.culturalNote: Finding landmarks in Maharashtra. Marathi addresses often use 'peths' (areas) and landmarks like temples.

phrases.english: Please show me on the map

lessons.title: Giving Directions
lessons.culturalNote: Understanding directions given by locals. 'Davi' is left, 'Ujavi' is right.

phrases.english: The shop is on the left side
You will see a temple in front

lessons.title: Buying Tickets
lessons.culturalNote: Purchasing transit tickets for local trains (like Mumbai Locals) or state transport (MSRTC) buses.

phrases.english: Give me a ticket to Mumbai
How many tickets do you need?
How much is the ticket fare?
Where can I get a pass?

lessons.title: Modes of Transport
lessons.culturalNote: Identifying modes of transportation. 'Auto' or 'Rickshaw' is the most popular for short distances.

phrases.english: Does the rickshaw run on meter?

lessons.title: Travel Inquiries
lessons.culturalNote: Asking about schedules, delays, and routes.

phrases.english: When will the next train leave?
Does this bus go to Pune?
How many hours is the journey?

title: Daily Routine
description: Morning to night habits, chores, work and office routines

lessons.title: Morning Routine
lessons.culturalNote: Describing what you do in the morning. Use present habitual tense.

phrases.english: I wake up early in the morning (male)
I wake up early in the morning (female)

lessons.title: Household Chores
lessons.culturalNote: Talking about housekeeping activities around the house.

lessons.title: Evening & Bedtime
lessons.culturalNote: Winding down the day and preparing for bed.

phrases.english: I fell into a deep sleep

lessons.title: Work & Office Routine
lessons.culturalNote: Describing a typical professional workday.

phrases.marathi: माझे ऑफिस नऊ वाजता सुरू होते
phrases.transliteration: Maajhe office nau vaajataa suroo hote

phrases.marathi: मी पाच वाजता लॉग ऑफ करतो
phrases.transliteration: Mee paach vaajataa log off karto

lessons.title: Weekend Habits
lessons.culturalNote: What you do during holidays or weekends (weekly off).

phrases.english: On Saturdays I wake up late (male)

title: People & Descriptions
description: Physical attributes, personality, emotions, family and friends

lessons.title: Physical Appearance
lessons.culturalNote: Describing what someone looks like. Height, hair, and posture.

lessons.title: Describing Personality
lessons.culturalNote: Describing inner nature (svabhaav). 'Svabhaav' is a very key cultural word.

lessons.title: Expressing Emotions
lessons.culturalNote: Saying how you feel. Use present state verbs.

phrases.english: I am bored / tired of this

lessons.title: Relations & Friends
lessons.culturalNote: Describing family relations and close friends.

lessons.title: Comparisons
lessons.culturalNote: Comparing two people or things using 'pekshaa' (compared to).

phrases.english: This house is the most beautiful
